name: Idaho (United States)
description: A blue field with the Great Seal of Idaho centred upon it. Below the seal is a red scroll, edged and lettered in gold, bearing the words 'STATE OF IDAHO'.
history: Originally adopted on 15 March 1907 as a regimental flag for the Idaho infantry returning from the Spanish-American War; codified as the state flag of Idaho by an act of the legislature on 12 March 1927. The seal in the centre, designed by Emma Edwards Green in 1890-1891, is the only U.S. state seal designed by a woman.

features.role: Great Seal of the State of Idaho
features.symbolism: designed by Emma Edwards Green, the only U.S. state seal designed by a woman; it depicts a miner and a woman representing justice and equality, with mountains, agriculture and an elk's head

features.role: red scroll bearing the words 'STATE OF IDAHO' in gold beneath the seal
